frameworks.name: constraint-based causal discovery
frameworks.one_line: uses conditional independence between contemporaneous and lagged variables to recover causal links

frameworks.name: Granger causality
frameworks.one_line: time-lagged predictive causality that cannot model contemporaneous effects or latent common causes

frameworks.name: dynamic causal modeling
frameworks.one_line: generative biophysical model for effective connectivity but limited in handling contemporaneous structure

frameworks.name: causal structure learning
frameworks.one_line: extends DAG-style discovery to large-scale, low-resolution time series allowing feedback

core_question: Can a scalable causal-discovery method recover whole-brain causal connectomes from fMRI despite contemporaneous effects, latent confounders, and feedback?
key_assumptions: conditional independence patterns identify causal links in low-resolution time series
contemporaneous and lagged dependencies are both informative for causality
feedback (cyclic) structure must be permitted, not assumed away
macaque connectome provides ground truth for simulated benchmarking
manipulations: method compared across simple simulated fMRI topologies
key_findings: CaLLTiF achieves higher accuracy and scalability than tested alternatives on macaque simulations
learned human causal connectomes are highly consistent across individuals
top-down causal flow runs from attention and default mode to sensorimotor networks
causal interactions show Euclidean distance dependence and are dominated by contemporaneous effects
interpretations: contemporaneous effects are central to brain causal structure and must be modeled explicitly
constraint-based discovery enables practical whole-brain causal connectomics from fMRI
